title: hebe buxifolia
intro: Hebe buxifolia, a species of flowering plant native to New Zealand, is a member of the hebe family and is also known as the box-leaved hebe. The leaves and flowers of Hebe buxifolia have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing stress, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Hebe buxifolia can be prepared for medicinal use in various herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions.
